meta.topic: Reporting with passives; it is said that...

step_1_context.text: We often use a passive to report what people say, think, etc., particularly if it is not important to mention who is being reported:

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: We often use a passive to report what people say, think, etc.
step_2_explanation.formula: Another common way of reporting what is said by an unspecified group of people is to use it + passive verb + that-clause (see Unit 33 for more on that-clauses).
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: An alternative to it + passive verb + that-clause is to use subject + passive verb + to-infinitive if we want the subject to be the topic of the sentence (see Unit 22C).

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 25.1.1 It was ___ to hold new negotiations next month.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Both agree and announce can be used with it + passive + to-infinitive clause.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 25.1.2 It has been ___ that the crash was the result of pilot error.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Show and reveal are both possible here.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 25.1.3 It was ___ that Mrs Ho would chair the meeting.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Hope and reveal are both possible; explain is not used in Pattern 1.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 25.1.4 It has been ___ to appoint Dr Ahmadi as head teacher.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Decide and propose can be followed by a to-infinitive clause.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 25.1.6 It has now been ___ that half of cancer cases are lifestyle-related.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Reveal, establish, and discover all work for Pattern 1.
